verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, with cytosolic catalytic and SH2/SH3 domains facing the cytoplasm — not accessible from the extracellular side at baseline. However, ecto-SRC / surface-displayed SRC has been reported on tumor cells and activated platelets, consistent with cancer-state ecto-kinase display documented for several Src-family and related kinases. This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility. Not tissue-restricted germline display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented constitutive PM-outer-leaflet cycling pool; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering described.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of evidence for tumor-cell ecto-SRC surface display is limited; baseline is firmly inner-leaflet cytoplasmic.
